global.title: Tesla finally launches a bike — but it’s not what fans wanted
global.summary: Tesla fans have spent years begging the company to build an e-bike. Tesla just launched a bike — and it has no motor, no pedals, and it’s for toddlers. The new “Balance Bike for Kids” sells for $225 on Tesla’s online shop, and it’s aimed at children aged 2 to 5.

global.title: The BMW iX4 looks more like a grand tourer than an SUV [Images]
global.summary: The 2027 iX4 is set to join BMW’s electric vehicle lineup soon, bringing a unique new look to the family. Ahead of its debut, the 2027 BMW iX4 was spotted on the road in Germany.

global.title: Tesla (TSLA) shareholders are begging Musk to explain missed goals
global.summary: Tesla shareholders have flooded the company’s Q2 2026 earnings Q&A portal with questions, and the most-upvoted ones tell a bleak story: retail investors are now demanding that Elon Musk explain why the company keeps missing its own short-term goals. The tone has shifted from asking about Tesla’s gra

global.title: Kia EV2 Long Range goes on sale with 281 miles of range, plus GT and Black-Line trims
global.summary: The Kia EV2 is now available with a larger battery providing up to 281 miles of driving range, plus a few new trim options.

global.title: Xpeng’s new L03 undercuts Tesla Model Y by up to $10,000 in Europe
global.summary: Xpeng launched its new L03 electric SUV-coupe in Europe this week with a starting price of €34,990 — undercutting the Tesla Model Y by roughly €3,400 in Germany and by around $10,000 in Norway. The pricing puts a well-equipped 4.65-meter electric SUV directly under Tesla’s best-seller in its highest

global.title: Segway Max G3 e-scooter at $1,000 2026 low, EcoFlow dual-bundle power station flash sale, Tapo solar security camera 3-pack low, more
global.summary: Leading today’s Green Deals are a special promotion on Segway’s Max G3 Electric Scooter at a $1,000 2026 low for members (sign-up is free). Right behind it, we have an EcoFlow flash sale that is offering double bundles of power stations, expansion batteries, and more – all starting from $879. There’

global.title: Honda is officially pulling the plug on its only EV in the US
global.summary: The Honda Prologue is still among America’s top 10 best-selling electric vehicles, but it won’t be for much longer.

global.title: Volvo delivers first EX60 EVs, a ‘game changer’ with 500+ miles range and fast charging
global.summary: The EX60 is a true “game changer” for the Volvo brand, with the first customer deliveries now underway.

global.title: Chip Motors unveils $15,000 electric ‘life utility vehicle’
global.summary: Miami-based startup Chip Motors has unveiled Chip, an electric “life utility vehicle” that starts at $15,000 and tops out at 25 mph. The boxy, open-air four-seater is a low-speed vehicle aimed at short daily trips, and it lands in one of the fastest-growing corners of the EV market.

global.title: XPeng L03 ships Google Maps Auto SDK — a first for an APAC automaker
global.summary: XPeng used the global launch of its new L03 in Munich to announce that the electric SUV coupe is the first vehicle from any Asia-Pacific automaker to ship with Google Maps’ Auto SDK built in.
